lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Greetings. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: I'm conducting important business here for the Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: There is something I could use some help with. I'm here on behalf of the Kourend Council to investigate some unusual happenings in Port Piscarilius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you investigating?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Port Piscarilius is home to various gangs. They're a nuisance, but up until now we haven't had much trouble in dealing with them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's the problem?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: A new gang calling themselves the Saviours of Kourend has recently formed. That's not surprising as gangs come and go all of the time, but this one is different.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: The Saviours of Kourend have been absorbing many of the other gangs. Within a few weeks they've already become the largest gang in Kourend and more join them each day.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: The talk amongst the people is that it's all down to their leader, a mysterious figure known only as the Queen of Thieves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice name. Why doesn't the ruler of Port Piscarilius do something about this?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Shauna Piscarilius is the ruler of Port Piscarilius, but she spends most of her time galavanting around in that boat of hers rather than looking after her home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. So what do you need me for?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Well my original plan was to infiltrate the gang to get close to the Queen and capture her. However, I've been unable to make any progress. Apparently, I don't look the part.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah not sure what that's all about. You clearly fit right in here.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Quite right. They're quite insistent though, so someone else will need to give it a go. You look like you might be just the type they're after.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you trying to say?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: That you look poor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Anyway if you're up for the task you'll need to infiltrate the Saviours of Kourend, find the Queen of Thieves, apprehend her and then bring her to me.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: So are you up for it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Excellent. The Saviours of Kourend must have their headquarters somewhere nearby. You should ask around town and see if anyone knows where they are. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nah.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Fair enough. I'll be here if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the Kourend Council?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Although it's called a kingdom, Great Kourend hasn't actually had a king for many years. These days Kourend is a republic and it is ruled by the Kourend Council.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: The Council consists of four councillors who are elected by the people every four years. The current councillors are Councillor Hughes, Councillor Unkar, Councillor Orson and Councillor Andrews.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So what are you investigating?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Port Piscarilius is home to various gangs. They're a nuisance, but up until now we haven't had much trouble in dealing with them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's the problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a quest.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Well there is something I could use some help with. However, you're not really the person I need for this task.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Well there is something I could use some help with. I'm here on behalf of the Kourend Council to investigate some unusual happenings in Port Piscarilius.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well good luck with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Very well. Until next time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Greetings. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my quest.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Very well. How goes things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Well come back to me once you have the Queen of Thieves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Greetings. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my quest.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Very well. How goes things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't had any luck finding the Queen of Thieves. However, I've come across this letter which you might find interesting.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Blimey! Forget the Queen of Thieves. If this letter is to be believed, Councillor Hughes is working with those vile Disciples of Yama. I must report this to the council immediately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! So can I have a reward?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: You've done a great service to the people of Kourend. As I'm sure you know, there is no greater reward than the satisfaction of a job well done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Now be on your way. I have a lot of work to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know of the Disciples of Yama?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: They're a group of deranged cultists that worship some sort of demon. They're not a big threat compared to the lizardmen, but they're still an enemy to Kourend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And Councillor Hughes is helping them? That's not good.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Indeed. I must report this to the Council immediately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I have a reward?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: You've done a great service to the people of Kourend. As I'm sure you know, there is no greater reward than the satisfaction of a job well done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Now be on your way. I have a lot of work to do.

sections.title: After The Queen of Thieves and before A Kingdom Divided

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Tomas. How's things?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Very well thank you. My work in bringing down Councillor Hughes means that I'm finally starting to get the recognition that I deserve.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But that was my work!

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Yes, yes, very good job. I'll make sure to mention you in my acceptance speech when I'm voted in as her replacement.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Tomas.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Oh... Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something wrong?

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: I'm ruined. Everything was perfect under the Council, but now I'm right back in the dirt again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You were very close to the Council. You should be glad you got to keep your job at all.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: But I didn't keep my job. Now I spend every day doing boring administrative work. It's a nightmare.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Come now Tomas, it can't be all bad. Show a little enthusiasm.

lines.speaker: Tomas Lawry
lines.text: Eugh.
